compiled_state.title: Sprint orchestrator suggests epic closure when completion verifier returns FAIL
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
The sprint orchestrator (Phase 7) presented the completion verifier's FAIL verdict alongside rationalizations for why each failing criterion could be waived, then asked the user whether to proceed with closure. This is incorrect — a FAIL from the completion verifier must block epic closure until the failing criteria are resolved.

## Actual Behavior
The orchestrator received VERIFIER_RESULT: FAIL with 3 failing criteria (SC1, SC2, SC8), then:
- Classified SC1 (Ruby/Synvert missing) as 'intent_gap / never in any story / Epic B scope'
- Classified SC2 (executor rollback delegated to adapters) as 'implementation_gap, ticket created'
- Classified SC8 (synthetic fixtures, not real public repo) as 'borderline intent/implementation'
Then presented these rationalizations to the user and asked: 'Before I close epic dso-qo0x, should I proceed with closure noting these tracked gaps?'

## Rationalization embedded in the behavior
The orchestrator reasoned that (a) Ruby was never in a story so it was descoped, (b) rollback works via adapters so the deviation is acceptable, (c) synthetic fixtures were a conscious choice. These rationalizations are all plausible — but the completion verifier exists precisely to be an impartial check. When an orchestrator can rationalize around a FAIL verdict, the gate provides no protection.

## Expected Behavior
When VERIFIER_RESULT: FAIL, the sprint orchestrator must route all failing criteria to remediation (implementation_gap → implementation-plan, intent_gap → brainstorm) and MUST NOT suggest closing the epic until a subsequent verifier run returns PASS.

## Fix
Sprint SKILL.md Phase 7 Step 0.75 should explicitly state: 'If VERIFIER_RESULT: FAIL, do NOT suggest or proceed with epic closure. Route to remediation. Re-run the verifier after remediation completes. Closure is only possible after VERIFIER_RESULT: PASS.'

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral — ticket describes sprint orchestrator behavioral failure AND fix requires editing plugins/dso/skills/sprint/SKILL.md
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Behavioral assertion (LLM-behavioral exemption): When dso:completion-verifier returns FAIL in Phase 7, sprint orchestrator MUST block epic closure and route back to Phase 3. Current behavior: orchestrator presents rationalizations and offers user choice. Expected: FAIL is unconditionally blocking — no user dialog about waiving criteria.
comments.author: Test
